PRO HAC VICE RENEWAL FEE STATUS FOR ATTORNEY JENNY J. ZHANG
Off Calendar May-12-2022 Case Transferred

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-09-2021 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-08-2021 Continued To Dec-29-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO TRANSFER
Off Calendar Aug-23-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ALFREDO W. AMOEDO, Esq. of Haynes and Boone, LLP (Tel: 650-687-8800) for defendant Maryna Petrenko, d/b/a DNI Events;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT MARYNA PETRENKO, D/B/A DNI EVENTS' MOTION TO TRANSFER.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to transfer venue to Sonoma County is granted. Plaintiff shall pay all transfer costs and fees. The August 16, 2021 amendment to the parties' joint stipulation and order for stay of temporary restraining order shall remain in effet pending the transfer of this action.

"When several causes of action are alleged in a complaint, a motion for change of venue must be granted on all causes if defendant is entitled to a change on any one." (Johnson v. Superior Court (1965) 232 Cal.App.2d 212, 217.) For Defendant, an individual, the general venue rule applies: "the county where the defendants or some of them reside at the commencement of the action is the proper court for the trial of the action." (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 395(a).) Plaintiff's argument that because DNI is named in the complaint, venue is proper pursuant to section 395.5 of the Code of Civil Procedure, which governs venue in actions against corporations, is rejected. The complaint is brought against "Defendant Maryna Petrenko . . . an individual and former employee . . . ." (Compl. 3.)

Plaintiff does not show that the exception for injury to personal property under section 395(a) of the Code of Civil Procedure applies to all of the causes of action. Rather, courts have held that it does not apply to claims for conversion. "The general rule is that the residence of defendant is the proper place for trial, and this applies to personal torts not involving physical injury including conversion." (Haurat v. Superior Court (1966) 241 Cal.App.2d 330, 333 (collecting cases); see also, e.g., Mason v. Buck (1929) 99 Cal.App. 219, 221 ["[T]he Legislature intended to except from the general provisions of section 395 only such actions . . . . As an action for conversion is not a corporeal injury to property within this meaning, it would follow that it should be tried in the county where the defendant resides within the general provisions of section 395."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
